summary:
During tooth tipping, periodontal ligament (PDL) fibers undergo asymmetric mechanical stress due to their attachment sites and directional movement—some experiencing tension while others face compression. This reflects an imbalance where fibbers are partially stretched and partially compressed, consistent with theoretical biomechanical principles supporting answer choice C. 

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{C}}$
